name: Naba Rizvi
summary: I am a multi-award-winning responsible AI researcher using quantitative metrics, participatory data collection, empirical studies, and other data-driven methods to de-bias and evaluate AI models and agents. I have first-author publications at ACL, FAccT, CHI, and CSCW, grants from Google and NCWIT, and research internships at Google AI, Microsoft, and Adobe leading to publications and product impact. Invited speaker for panels at major conferences, including NeurIPS, SigCSE, Tapia, and GHC.

employment_history.position: Graduate Student Researcher, UC San Diego
details: Developed a novel benchmark dataset to measure & mitigate anti-autistic bias in AI, fine-tuned & tested 10 pre-trained LLMs
Creating a novel bias-aware weighting scheme for annotation labels to improve accuracy and efficiency

Measured anti-autistic bias in a systematic critical review of HRI research as a case study in applied AI research, and provided community-generated recommendations for more inclusive research practices
Disseminated my research as an invited interviewee for 6 national and international media organizations, and a multimedia content creator on TikTok, Instagram, and YouTube with over 12,000 views
Three first-author full papers for FAccT, ACL, and CSCW in review
Published full first-author paper quantifying anti-autistic bias at CHI 2024
Refined & tested comparison and scale-based annotation schemes to increase human-AI alignment in skin type labeling for DermAssist
Increased the efficiency of data labeling 2x, improved agreement among dermatologists and non-expert annotators
Conducted a comprehensive mixed methods study with qualitative observations and semi-structured interviews
Examined data using descriptive statistics, correlations, inferential tests, and a qualitative thematic analysis
Designed technologies making implicit biases toward minority patients explicit to healthcare providers
Identified and quantified interruptions in speech using social signals, and conducted wireframe usability testing
First-author short paper, and co-authored late breaking paper presented @ CHI 2022
Led the QTBIPOC PD workshop as a first-author at CHI 2022, and obtained $3,000 in funding from Google
Developed and deployed an online course promoting autism inclusion, used to train thousands of employees
Created a likert scale survey with open-ended questions, conducted qualitative & quantitative analysis of data
First-author full paper presented at AMCIS 2021
Made speed reading accessible for Blind and Low-Vision users by generating paragraph-based summaries
Developed the alpha version of web app using an NLP model for the backend, and conducted a needs-finding assessment through surveys
First-author short paper presented at INLG 2019

education.degree: Ph.D., University of California, San Diego, Computer Science
education.notes: Thesis topic: De-Biasing Neuronormative AI through Data-Driven and Participatory Approaches
